title: Peptide-MHC Class I Binding Affinity Prediction

target.name: target
target.description: Transformed MHC class I binding affinity in [0, 1] computed as 1 - log(IC50_nM)/log(50000); higher means tighter binding.

feature_descriptions.peptide: Nine-residue candidate peptide in one-letter amino-acid code.
feature_descriptions.allele: HLA class I allele name in NetMHCpan-style four-digit form (e.g. HLA-A0201).
feature_descriptions.allele_pseudo_seq: 34-residue HLA pseudo-sequence (NetMHCpan convention) covering the peptide-binding groove. Shipped for convenience so participants do not need an external HLA lookup.
feature_descriptions.target: Training label: transformed binding affinity 1 - log(IC50_nM)/log(50000), clipped to [0, 1].

domain_background: Allele-cold MHC class I binding-affinity prediction derived from MHCflurry's curated IEDB export. The six held-out HLA alleles do not appear in training; each has at least one same-locus sibling allele in the training set so generalization across the binding groove is feasible.
allowed_external_resources: Use public scientific databases, package documentation, and pretrained protein or peptide-MHC models. Do not use hidden labels or scorer files.
